quiz.title: Unit 12 Quiz 12, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Air Pollution and Atmospheric Chemistry
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Greenhouse Gas Sources, Acid Rain, Ozone Depletion, Smog Formation, Indoor Air Pollution, Primary vs Secondary Pollutants

questions.question: The main source of nitrous oxide (N₂O) emissions is:
questions.explanation: Agricultural activities, particularly the use of nitrogen-based fertilizers and manure management, are the primary sources of nitrous oxide emissions. Soil management accounts for about 75% of N₂O emissions.

questions.question: Which sector contributes most to global CO₂ emissions?
questions.explanation: Electricity and heat production (primarily from burning coal and natural gas) is the largest source of global CO₂ emissions, accounting for about 40% of total emissions.

questions.question: Which natural process is the largest carbon sink?
questions.explanation: Oceans are the largest carbon sink, absorbing about 25-30% of human-produced CO₂ emissions. They have absorbed approximately 30% of the CO₂ released since the industrial revolution.

questions.question: Acid rain is primarily caused by which gases?
questions.explanation: Sulfur dioxide (SO₂) and nitrogen oxides (NOₓ) react with water, oxygen, and other chemicals in the atmosphere to form sulfuric and nitric acids, which fall as acid rain.

questions.question: Which gas depletes the stratospheric ozone layer?
questions.explanation: Chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs) release chlorine atoms in the stratosphere, which catalytically destroy ozone molecules. The Montreal Protocol has successfully phased out most CFCs.

questions.question: Smog in urban areas are formed when organic compounds (VOCs) and _______ react in sunlight.
questions.explanation: Photochemical smog forms when nitrogen oxides (NOx) and volatile organic compounds (VOCs) react in the presence of sunlight, producing ground-level ozone and other harmful pollutants.

questions.question: Indoor air pollution from cooking with solid fuels primarily involves:
questions.explanation: Burning solid fuels (wood, coal, biomass) indoors produces carbon monoxide (CO) and fine particulate matter, which are major causes of respiratory diseases in developing countries.

questions.question: Which gas is responsible for the 'rotten egg' smell and ecosystem acidification?
questions.explanation: Hydrogen sulfide (H₂S) has a characteristic rotten egg odor and can contribute to acidification when oxidized to sulfur compounds. SO₂ is more directly responsible for acid rain.

questions.question: The main health effect of ground-level ozone is:
questions.explanation: Ground-level ozone is a powerful respiratory irritant that can cause coughing, throat irritation, and damage to lung tissue. It exacerbates asthma and other respiratory conditions.

questions.question: Flaring of natural gas (methane) at oil wells converts it to:
questions.explanation: Flaring is the controlled burning of natural gas, converting methane to carbon dioxide and water vapor. While CO₂ is still a greenhouse gas, it has a lower global warming potential than methane.

questions.question: Select all that apply: Which gas is both an asphyxiant and toxic at high concentrations?
questions.explanation: CO₂ can displace oxygen (asphyxiant) and cause toxicity at high levels. CO binds to hemoglobin (toxic) and displaces oxygen. SO₂ is toxic to respiratory system. N₂ is only an asphyxiant (displaces O₂) but not toxic.

questions.question: Select all that apply: Volcanic eruptions release which climate-affecting gases?
questions.explanation: Volcanoes release significant amounts of SO₂ (which forms sulfate aerosols cooling the climate) and CO₂ (greenhouse gas). NO₂ and CH₄ are not major volcanic emissions.

questions.question: The layer of the atmosphere that contains the ozone layer is known as _____________
questions.explanation: The ozone layer is located in the stratosphere, approximately 15-35 km above Earth's surface. It absorbs 93-99% of harmful UV radiation from the sun.

questions.question: The main source of carbon monoxide (CO) in the atmosphere is ___________
questions.explanation: Carbon monoxide is primarily produced by incomplete combustion of fossil fuels in vehicles, power plants, and industrial processes. It is also produced by biomass burning.

questions.question: Which of the following pollutants is a secondary air pollutant?
questions.explanation: Ozone is a secondary pollutant formed when primary pollutants (NOx and VOCs) react in sunlight. SO₂, NOx, and CO are primary pollutants emitted directly from sources.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered air pollution and atmospheric chemistry! 🌍🧪
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review sources of different pollutants and their effects.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on distinguishing primary vs secondary pollutants.
scoreMessages.0: Please review air pollution sources, greenhouse gases, and atmospheric layers.
